nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Meriet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Merritt
meanings: English: variant of Merriott , Marriott , or Marritt. The last of these is most often a variant of Merriott or Marriott , but possibly in some instances from Middle English Marret or Merret, a syncopated form of Margaret, Mergeret.

nameMeanings.name: Cecilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

variants.name: Cecelia
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet was born about 1307, in Devon, England. She married Sir John Dodiscomb about 1327, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. She died in Compton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet est née environ 1307, à Devon, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir John Dodiscomb environ 1327, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée à Compton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet wurde um 1307 in Devon, England geboren. Sie hat um 1327 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Sir John Dodiscomb geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Sie starb in Compton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet è nata il circa 1307 nel Devon, England. Lei sposato Sir John Dodiscomb circa 1327, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Morì nel Compton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Merietは約1307年にイギリスデボンで生まれた。約1327年にイギリスイギリスデボンでSir John Dodiscombと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスデボンコンプトンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet(은)는 대략 1307년 영국 데폰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1327년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Sir John Dodiscomb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 콤프턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet nasceu aproximadamente 1307, em Devon, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir John Dodiscomb aproximadamente 1327, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em Compton, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet родилась около 1307 г. в местности девон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Dodiscomb около 1327 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в местности комптон, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cecilia De Meriet nació aproximadamente 1307, en Devon, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir John Dodiscomb aproximadamente 1327, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas. Murió en Compton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
